regions.us-ports: Today, winds in Charleston are light from the south-southwest at 5 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero percent chance of rain, allowing smooth port operations and minimal impact on shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect stronger westerly winds at 18 knots with overcast conditions and a slight 9 percent rain chance, which may affect embarkation and disembarkation schedules at Charleston and nearby ports such as Savannah and Norfolk. By the second day, east-northeast winds will ease to 10 knots with clear skies and no rain expected, improving conditions for vessels docking in Miami, Port Everglades, and Port Canaveral. Beyond 48 hours, easterly winds will increase to 12 knots with overcast skies but no rain, so crews should prepare for reduced visibility during operations at Gulf and East Coast ports including New Orleans and Jacksonville. Passengers should remain flexible with shore excursion timing as these evolving wind and cloud trends may cause minor scheduling adjustments.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today conditions across the Bahamas and Central Caribbean ports including Nassau, Freeport, Bimini, and Key West feature light easterly winds around 5 to 6 knots with clear to overcast skies and minimal rain chances near 1 to 5 percent, supporting normal port operations and shore excursions with good visibility and calm seas. Over the next 24 to 48 hours expect elevated winds increasing to NNE 21 knots by Day 2 with overcast skies and a slight rain chance near 10 percent impacting Nassau, Freeport, Bimini, and Key West; this will require caution during tender operations and may cause moderate sea states affecting passenger transfers and some sensitive shore excursions. By Day 3, winds will further strengthen to NE 26 knots with drizzle and rain probabilities rising to 33 percent in Nassau and surrounding ports including Grand Cayman, Ocho Rios, and La Romana, potentially leading to Small Craft Advisories and reduced visibility complicating embarkation and disembarkation procedures. Beyond 48 hours, expect these elevated wind conditions to persist with continued drizzle and increasing rain chances, especially near Santo Domingo and Samaná, advising all vessels to prepare for sustained rougher seas and adjust itineraries accordingly to maintain passenger safety and operational efficiency.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today, easterly winds at 6 knots and partly cloudy skies with only a 15% chance of rain will support smooth port operations and shore excursions across San Juan, St. Thomas, St. Croix, St. Maarten, St. Kitts, and Antigua. Expect mostly dry conditions with good visibility for embarkation and disembarkation activities at all ports. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, sustained easterly winds will increase to 13 knots accompanied by overcast skies and a higher rain probability of 35% today, particularly impacting San Juan and St. Thomas, where intermittent showers may cause brief delays in outdoor excursions. On the second day, winds remain easterly at 13 knots with overcast skies but a reduced 13% rain chance, improving conditions at St. Croix, St. Maarten, and Antigua. Beyond 48 hours, winds shift slightly to east-northeast at 12 knots with continued overcast conditions and a moderate 22% chance of rain, suggesting persistent but manageable cloud cover and isolated showers across the Eastern Caribbean ports.
regions.western-caribbean: Today, Cozumel, Costa Maya, Roatan, and Belize City are experiencing generally stable conditions with light ENE winds at 6 knots and overcast skies, and no rain expected, so port operations and shore excursions are proceeding without weather delays. In the next 24 to 48 hours, expect increasing northeast winds up to 14 knots with overcast skies and an elevated 48% chance of rain on April 20, which will most affect Costa Maya and Belize City shore activities due to higher rain probabilities. By April 21, winds shift slightly ENE at 13 knots with a 69% chance of rain showers, signaling likely interruptions to embarkation and disembarkation in Cozumel and Roatan. Passengers and crew should anticipate reduced visibility and wet decks during these periods and plan accordingly for possible outdoor excursion adjustments. Beyond 48 hours, the persistent cloud cover and increasing rain chances require ongoing monitoring for changes in sea state and port access conditions.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today in the Southern Caribbean, expect moderate easterly winds at 12 knots in Aruba with partly cloudy skies and an 18% chance of rain, allowing normal port operations at Aruba, Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena. Shore excursions remain largely unaffected today given the low rain probability and stable conditions, although crews should monitor localized showers. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, the region will see an increase to 14-knot east winds with rain showers forecasted at a 35% probability on April 20, particularly impacting Aruba and Curacao with overcast skies and intermittent rain that may delay some outdoor excursions and affect visibility during port operations. By April 21, conditions are expected to improve with steady east winds at 14 knots, partly cloudy skies, and a reduced 23% rain chance, signaling better weather for safe embarkation and disembarkation at all Southern Caribbean ports. Passengers and crew should stay alert for these rain showers tomorrow and plan accordingly for potential minor delays in shore activities at Aruba and Curacao.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today, winds out of the east-northeast are steady at 14 knots with partly cloudy skies and only a 20% chance of rain, allowing normal port operations and shore excursions across Barbados, St. Lucia, Martinique, Dominica, Antigua, St. Kitts, St. Maarten, St. Vincent, and Grenada with minimal weather impacts. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect winds to maintain at 15 knots ENE with overcast skies and a slight increase in rain probability to around 23-28%, which may cause some light delays or intermittent disruptions to outdoor activities particularly in Barbados, St. Lucia, and Martinique. Ports such as Antigua, St. Kitts, and St. Maarten will also see similar overcast conditions with moderate rain chances, requiring crew to monitor docking and tender operations for wet decks. Beyond 48 hours, winds are forecast to ease slightly to 12 knots ENE with continued overcast skies and a reduced 15% chance of rain, signaling improving conditions for port calls and shore excursions in the Lesser Antilles region. Passengers and crew should remain prepared for variable visibility and slick surfaces during embarkation and disembarkation through this period.
regions.los-angeles: Today, winds are light from the SSE at 2 knots with overcast skies and zero percent rain chance, allowing smooth embarkation and disembarkation at Los Angeles / San Pedro, Long Beach, Marina del Rey, and Catalina Island with minimal operational impact. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect WSW winds increasing to 11 knots with persistent overcast conditions and a near zero to one percent chance of rain, which should keep port operations steady across all regional terminals. By day three, winds shift slightly to the SW at 12 knots, accompanied by drizzle and a 26 percent chance of rain, likely impacting shore excursions especially at Catalina Island where wet conditions may reduce visibility and footing on outdoor tours. Passengers and crew should prepare for this developing trend of light rain and moderate winds beyond 48 hours, which may require adjustments to outdoor activities and minor operational delays at all ports. No wind warnings or severe weather threats are forecast, so vessel maneuvering and passenger movements should remain routine with close attention to drizzle conditions near Catalina.
regions.ensenada: Today in the Ensenada region, winds are light from the northeast at 1 knot under an overcast sky with zero percent chance of rain, allowing smooth port operations and shore excursions at Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos without weather disruptions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect westerly winds increasing to 9 knots with continued overcast conditions and no rain chance, maintaining favorable conditions for embarkation and disembarkation activities at all three ports. By 72 hours, winds will shift slightly to the west-southwest at 9 knots with a 15 percent chance of rain, primarily affecting Islas Todos Santos where light showers could impact outdoor excursions. Passengers and crew should monitor this developing trend beyond two days as increased cloud cover and the slight rain probability may require adjustments to onshore plans. Overall, current and near-term weather supports safe cruise operations with only minor potential impacts by day three.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Today, winds out of the north-northwest at 12 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero percent chance of rain are supporting smooth port operations in Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz, allowing shore excursions to proceed without weather delays. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect northwest winds steady at 14 knots and a transition from partly cloudy to overcast skies, maintaining zero percent rain probability, which will continue to favor safe embarkation and disembarkation across all three ports. By day three, northwest winds will increase slightly to 15 knots with persistent overcast conditions and no rain, requiring crew to monitor sea state for any minor impacts on tendering operations especially at La Paz. There are no advisories or warnings, so cruise vessel maneuvering and passenger activities should remain routine throughout this period. Passengers and crew should remain alert for any sudden changes, but current data indicates stable marine weather conditions through April 21.
regions.mazatlan: Today in the Mazatlan region, winds are light from the northwest at 5 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero chance of rain, creating smooth conditions for embarkation and shore excursions at Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata ports. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect winds to shift slightly to WNW at 9 to 10 knots, maintaining partly cloudy skies and no rain, which will keep port operations steady across all three ports without weather-related delays. By day three, winds will become westerly at 10 knots with overcast skies but still no precipitation, possibly reducing sunshine during shore excursions in Mazatlan and Topolobampo while maintaining calm seas for safe vessel maneuvering. There are no advisories or warnings issued at this time, and the steady wind speeds below Small Craft Advisory thresholds indicate no impact to navigation or tender operations throughout this period. Passengers and crew should prepare for consistent light to moderate westerly winds and mainly dry conditions through the next 72 hours.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Today in the Puerto Vallarta region winds are light from the northwest at 1 knot with overcast skies and only a 3 percent chance of rain, so port operations at Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala are expected to proceed smoothly with minimal impact on embarkation, disembarkation, and shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will increase slightly to 7 to 8 knots from the west-northwest while skies remain overcast with the same low 3 percent rain chance, meaning all four ports will continue to experience stable conditions with no significant weather-related delays. Beyond 48 hours, skies will begin to clear to partly cloudy while west-northwest winds hold steady at 7 knots and rain chances remain at 3 percent, allowing for improved visibility and continued safe operations throughout the region. There are no marine wind warnings or elevated rain probabilities to disrupt cruise vessel maneuvers or passenger activities in any port over the next three days. Passengers and crew should expect consistent light winds and mostly overcast to partly cloudy skies with negligible precipitation through April 21.
regions.western-mediterranean: Today, winds across the Western Mediterranean ports including Barcelona and Valencia are light from the west-southwest at 1 knot with partly cloudy skies and zero percent rain chance, ensuring smooth port operations and ideal conditions for all shore excursions. Looking ahead to the next 24 to 48 hours, expect a strengthening southerly breeze reaching up to 10 knots from the south-southwest with overcast skies and minimal rain probability near 3 percent, which may slightly reduce visibility in ports such as Palma de Mallorca, Ibiza, and Malaga but will not significantly impact embarkation or disembarkation. By day three, winds will shift to a southeast direction at 7 knots with continued overcast conditions and no rain expected, which will maintain steady conditions for cruise vessel maneuvering and port logistics at Cadiz, Lisbon, and Gibraltar. Passengers and crew should note this developing trend of persistent overcast skies combined with gentle southerly to southeasterly winds, which will require routine monitoring but do not currently pose any operational threats. We will continue to provide updates should wind speeds approach Small Craft Advisory thresholds or if any rain probabilities increase.
regions.central-mediterranean: Today in the Central Mediterranean, conditions in Naples feature light SSE winds at 2 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero chance of rain, supporting smooth port operations and uninterrupted shore excursions in Naples, as well as Marseille, Nice, Monaco, and Genoa. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect a shift to SSW winds increasing to 8 knots with continued partly cloudy skies and no rain, maintaining favorable conditions for embarkation and disembarkation at Civitavecchia, Livorno, and La Spezia. By day two, southern ports including Naples and Sardinia will experience southerly winds near 9 knots accompanied by fog reducing visibility, which may cause minor delays in tender operations and shore excursions. Beyond 48 hours, winds will strengthen slightly to 10 knots from the south with overcast skies and a 3% chance of rain near Naples and Corsica, so crews should monitor for possible changes in visibility and plan accordingly for passenger safety during outdoor activities. No marine wind warnings are in effect, and sea state should remain calm throughout the region.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today in the Eastern Mediterranean, conditions at Athens (Piraeus) show light northerly winds at 2 knots with clear skies and zero chance of rain, supporting smooth port operations and shore excursions with excellent visibility. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will shift to WNW at 6 knots with overcast skies in Athens and similar conditions expected in nearby ports such as Santorini and Mykonos, though no rain is forecasted, so embarkation and disembarkation should remain uninterrupted. By day three, winds will veer to a southerly direction at 7 knots with clear skies returning, which will benefit ports including Rhodes, Izmir, and Cyprus by improving sea conditions and visibility for outdoor activities. No marine wind warnings or rainfall threats are present through this period, allowing cruise vessels to maintain regular schedules without weather-related delays. Passengers and crew should monitor for continued calm wind speeds and clear sky trends through the weekend for optimal cruising and port call experiences.
